text: The transverse colon is positioned cranially, while the small bowel is placed in the left lumbar region and pelvis. The assistant lifts the ileocecal junction, allowing entry into the mesocolic plane along the ileocolic pedicle. The second and third parts of the duodenum are exposed as dissection begins along the superior mesenteric vein and the origin of the ileocolic pedicle. The surgeon continues medial to lateral dissection, separating the mesocolon from the duodenum, pancreas, and right kidney.

The surgeon continues the caudal to cranial dissection along the superior mesenteric vein and ileocolic pedicle, clearing fatty tissue along the right border of the SMV and the uncinate process. The dissection progresses along the anterior plane of the pancreas.

The surgeon clips and transects the ileocolic vein, followed by the transection of the ileocolic artery while maintaining traction. Dissection proceeds cranially along the superior mesenteric vein and the anterior surface of the pancreas, exposing the gastrocolic trunk. The ileocolic artery is transected after clearing surrounding fatty tissue.

The surgeon dissects, clips, and transects the gastrocolic trunk, followed by dissection along the anterior surface of the pancreas and clipping of the anterior superior pancreaticoduodenal vein. The medial to lateral dissection progresses towards the right lateral abdominal wall, with the assistant lifting the transverse mesocolon. The next step involves layering tissue along the origin and right branch of the middle colic artery.

The surgeon transects the right branch of the middle colic artery and carefully dissects along the proximal gastrocolic trunk and right gastroepiploic vein, which is then transected to clear fibro fatty tissue. The line of transection at the transverse mesocolon and ileal mesentery is identified and dissected. The lesser sac is entered by cutting the gastrocolic ligament, followed by lateral dissection along the white line of Toldt to connect with the medial dissection.

The surgeon releases the final attachment of the specimen by dissecting along the right gastroepiploic artery and the second part of the duodenum. An extracorporeal side-to-side anastomosis is performed, completing the surgical procedure.
